benchmark_weaknesses: Generic template typography — no distinctive type hierarchy or custom font pairing
Copy relies on buzzwords ('earn weekly', 'future of AI') without specificity or proof
No visible performance optimisation — likely unoptimised images and render-blocking resources
Shallow trust signals — lacks case studies, named customers, or verifiable social proof
Predictable layout patterns — hero + feature grid + CTA with no spatial personality
meta.audit_goal: Ensure every dimension of Meridian AI's website measurably exceeds Venturian AI's quality bar across design, performance, copy, accessibility, and trust.

checks.name: Distinctive visual identity system
checks.description: The site must have a coherent design system — colour tokens, spacing scale, and component library — that feels purpose-built, not template-derived.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian uses stock template colours and generic component styling with no identifiable design system.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Documented colour palette with ≤5 primary tokens, consistent 8px spacing scale applied site-wide, and no off-brand UI components.

checks.name: Non-generic hero composition
checks.description: The hero section must have a spatial layout that breaks from the centred-headline + subtext + CTA template pattern.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian's hero is a textbook centred-layout template with no compositional originality.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Hero uses asymmetric layout, editorial grid, or a compelling visual device that is not replicable by a generic builder.

checks.name: Consistent visual rhythm across sections
checks.description: Vertical spacing between sections must follow a defined rhythm — no jarring gaps or compressed sections.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently have inconsistent section padding as sections are stacked without a shared scale.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All section gaps fall within a 3-step spacing scale (e.g. 64px / 96px / 128px). No section gap deviates by more than 8px from the scale.

checks.name: No default drop shadows or gradients
checks.description: Gratuitous box-shadows and purple-to-blue gradients are hallmarks of generic AI site aesthetics and must be absent.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian-style sites lean heavily on neon gradients and card shadows to create perceived depth.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Zero instances of rgba box-shadow on cards, zero purple/blue hero gradients. Depth achieved through layout, whitespace, or typography weight alone.

checks.name: Mobile layout fidelity
checks.description: All sections must reflow gracefully to mobile without text overflow, broken grids, or horizontal scroll.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently have unoptimised mobile reflows with oversized headings and clipped elements.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Zero horizontal overflow at 375px viewport. All tap targets ≥44×44px. No text clipping below 320px width.

checks.name: Dark mode support
checks.description: The site must honour prefers-color-scheme and provide a polished dark variant that isn't just a colour inversion.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian has no dark mode support.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Verified dark mode at OS level on both Chrome and Safari. All text contrast ratios ≥4.5:1 in dark mode.

checks.name: Distinctive display typeface
checks.description: The primary heading font must be a deliberate, characterful choice — not a default system font or overused SaaS staple.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian uses Inter or a similar default SaaS font with no typographic personality.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Display font is not Inter, Roboto, Arial, or system-ui. Font choice is documented with a rationale in the design system.

checks.name: Clear 3-level type hierarchy
checks.description: H1, H2, body, and caption must be visually distinct in size, weight, and line-height with no ambiguity.
checks.benchmark_gap: Generic templates collapse H2 and H3 into near-identical sizes, flattening the hierarchy.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Minimum 1.25× size ratio between adjacent heading levels. Body line-height between 1.5–1.75. Caption ≤13px with distinct colour token.

checks.name: Controlled line length
checks.description: Body copy must stay within the optimal 60–80 character measure to ensure readability.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites stretch copy full-width on desktop creating unreadable 120+ character lines.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All body paragraphs have max-width that limits measure to 75ch or less on all viewport widths ≥1024px.

checks.name: No orphaned words in headings
checks.description: Short orphaned words at the end of heading lines are a polish signal — they must be eliminated.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template-generated copy rarely accounts for text reflow and leaves single-word orphans on all viewport sizes.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Zero heading orphans at 1440px, 1024px, and 768px viewports. Achieved via copy editing or CSS text-wrap: balance.

checks.name: Consistent font weight usage
checks.description: No more than 3 font weights used site-wide. Weight must encode meaning (body / emphasis / heading), not decoration.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites mix weights arbitrarily, using bold mid-sentence to add visual interest rather than semantic weight.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Audit confirms ≤3 weight tokens. No mid-paragraph bold that isn't a named entity, label, or genuinely critical term.

checks.name: Monospace font for code and technical strings
checks.description: Any API keys, code snippets, or technical strings must use a defined monospace face, not the default system mono.
checks.benchmark_gap: Generic sites render technical content in body font, signalling low developer credibility.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All <code> and technical inline elements use a defined mono font loaded via font-face or CDN.

checks.name: LCP under 2.5 seconds
checks.description: Largest Contentful Paint must meet Google's 'good' threshold to pass Core Web Vitals.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian-style marketing pages typically load hero images unoptimised, pushing LCP to 4–6s.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Lighthouse LCP ≤2.5s on mobile (simulated 4G throttle). Measured on 3 consecutive runs, median used.

checks.name: CLS under 0.1
checks.description: Cumulative Layout Shift must stay below the 'good' threshold to prevent jarring visual reflows.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites load web fonts without font-display:swap or size-adjust, causing large layout shifts.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Lighthouse CLS score ≤0.1 on 3 consecutive runs.

checks.name: Images in modern formats
checks.description: All raster images must be served in WebP or AVIF with appropriate srcset for responsive delivery.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites default to JPEG/PNG with no format optimisation or responsive image serving.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Zero PNG/JPEG images in production. All images use <picture> or srcset. Verified via Lighthouse image audit.

checks.name: No render-blocking resources
checks.description: CSS and JS must not block the critical rendering path. Fonts, scripts, and stylesheets must be loaded non-blocking.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template builders frequently inject render-blocking third-party scripts and inline CSS frameworks.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Zero render-blocking resources flagged by Lighthouse. All third-party scripts deferred or async.

checks.name: Total page weight under 1MB
checks.description: Uncompressed total transfer size for the landing page must stay lean.
checks.benchmark_gap: Marketing template sites frequently exceed 3MB due to unoptimised imagery and loaded-but-unused libraries.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Chrome DevTools Network panel shows total transfer ≤1MB on initial load with empty cache.

checks.name: Fonts subset and self-hosted
checks.description: Custom fonts must be subsetted to the character sets in use and served from the same origin to avoid DNS lookup latency.
checks.benchmark_gap: Generic sites load full Google Fonts payloads from external CDN, adding 200–400ms to font load time.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All fonts served from same origin. Font files subset to Latin character range. WOFF2 format only.

checks.name: No meaningless AI buzzwords
checks.description: Copy must be concrete and specific. Words like 'next-generation', 'cutting-edge', 'harness the power of' are banned.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian's tagline 'Earn weekly with the future of AI' is entirely buzzword-dependent and says nothing specific.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Copy audit returns zero instances of: next-generation, cutting-edge, harness, revolutionise, empower, unlock, leverage (as a verb).

checks.name: Hero communicates specific value in one sentence
checks.description: A first-time visitor must understand exactly what the product does and who it's for within 5 seconds of arriving.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian's hero is vague enough to describe any AI company — it communicates no specific differentiation.
checks.acceptance_criteria: User test with 5 cold participants: ≥4/5 can correctly describe the product's core function after a 5-second exposure.

checks.name: Feature copy leads with outcomes, not features
checks.description: Feature descriptions must lead with the benefit to the user, not the technical name of the capability.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites list features by their technical names without translating them to user outcomes.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All feature cards pass the 'so what?' test. Each card states a measurable or tangible outcome in the first sentence.

checks.name: CTA copy is specific and action-oriented
checks.description: Button copy must describe what happens next, not just 'Get started' or 'Learn more'.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian uses generic CTA copy ('Get started') that adds no conversion signal or expectation-setting.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Zero CTA buttons use: 'Get started', 'Learn more', 'Click here', 'Submit'. All CTAs describe the next step.

checks.name: Pricing copy is honest and complete
checks.description: If pricing is shown, all costs must be explicit. No 'starting from' without clarifying what increases the price.
checks.benchmark_gap: Typical AI marketing sites hide pricing complexity behind vague tier names and 'contact us' deflection.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All pricing tiers list included features, excluded features, and what triggers an upgrade. No asterisked conditions without inline explanation.

checks.name: Consistent brand voice across all copy
checks.description: Tone, sentence length, and vocabulary must be consistent across hero, features, testimonials, and footer.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites mix formal and casual tones across sections because copy was written in pieces.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Brand voice guide exists with ≥3 do/don't examples. Copy audit confirms no section violates documented tone guidelines.

checks.name: Page load animation is cohesive, not scattered
checks.description: If animations are used on page load, they must feel like a single choreographed sequence, not independent component animations firing randomly.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites apply generic fade-in to each section independently, creating an incoherent cascade.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Page load animation has a documented sequence with stagger timing. All elements enter within a 600ms window from first content paint.

checks.name: Hover states on all interactive elements
checks.description: Every button, link, and interactive card must have a visible hover state that communicates affordance.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently have links and buttons with no visual hover feedback.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Manual tab-through audit confirms every interactive element has a distinct :hover and :focus-visible state.

checks.name: No motion for motion's sake
checks.description: Every animation must serve a purpose: direct attention, show a state change, or communicate a relationship. Decorative-only animations are removed.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites use floating particles and auto-playing abstract animations that distract without informing.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Animation audit documents the purpose of every motion effect. All effects with no documented purpose are removed.

checks.name: Reduced motion support
checks.description: All animations must be disabled or substantially reduced when prefers-reduced-motion is active.
checks.benchmark_gap: Generic template sites ignore prefers-reduced-motion entirely.
checks.acceptance_criteria: prefers-reduced-motion: reduce disables all transform and opacity transitions site-wide. Verified in Chrome DevTools emulation.

checks.name: Scroll behaviour is native and smooth
checks.description: Anchor link scrolling must use CSS scroll-behavior: smooth, not a JS scroll library that can conflict with OS scroll settings.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template builders frequently implement custom JS scroll that overrides native smooth scrolling and breaks reduced-motion.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All anchor navigation uses CSS smooth scroll. No scroll-hijacking JS libraries present in the bundle.

checks.name: Form feedback is immediate and clear
checks.description: Any form submission must show a visible loading state, success state, and error state within 200ms of the triggering action.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template site forms frequently submit with no loading indicator and show vague or absent error messages.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All form states (loading, success, error) are visually distinct. Error messages identify the specific field and the required fix.

checks.name: WCAG 2.1 AA colour contrast throughout
checks.description: All text must meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio (body) and 3:1 (large text and UI components) against their background.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian-style sites frequently use light grey text on white backgrounds that fails AA contrast.
checks.acceptance_criteria: axe DevTools audit returns zero contrast violations. Manual check on all muted-text and caption-level elements.

checks.name: Full keyboard navigability
checks.description: Every interactive element must be reachable and operable via keyboard alone in a logical tab order.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently have modals and dropdown menus that trap keyboard focus or have no focus management.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Complete site navigation via Tab/Shift+Tab/Enter/Escape with no trapped focus. Tab order matches visual order.

checks.name: All images have meaningful alt text
checks.description: Decorative images must have empty alt attributes. Informational images must have descriptive alt text.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently leave alt attributes blank or use filename strings as alt text.
checks.acceptance_criteria: axe audit returns zero missing-alt violations. All decorative images confirmed with alt='' and role='presentation'.

checks.name: Semantic HTML structure
checks.description: Page must use correct heading hierarchy (single H1, logical H2/H3 nesting) and landmark elements (main, nav, footer).
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently use <div> soup with no landmark regions and broken heading hierarchies.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Single H1 per page. No skipped heading levels. Presence of <main>, <nav>, <footer> landmarks verified via accessibility tree.

checks.name: Focus visible on all interactive elements
checks.description: The default browser focus outline must not be suppressed without providing a custom, clearly visible replacement.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites commonly apply outline: none globally, removing focus visibility for keyboard users.
checks.acceptance_criteria: CSS audit confirms no outline: none or outline: 0 without an accompanying :focus-visible replacement. All focus indicators have 3:1 contrast against adjacent colours.

checks.name: ARIA used correctly and sparingly
checks.description: ARIA roles and attributes must only be used when native HTML semantics are insufficient. Incorrect ARIA is worse than no ARIA.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently add aria-label to elements that already have visible labels, creating duplicate announcements.
checks.acceptance_criteria: ARIA audit confirms no redundant aria-label on labelled form controls. No ARIA roles applied to non-interactive elements.

checks.name: Unique, descriptive title tags on every page
checks.description: Each page must have a <title> tag between 50–60 characters that includes the primary keyword and brand name.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites often use the same title tag across all pages or leave it as the builder default.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All pages have unique <title> tags 50–60 chars. No page shares a title with another. Brand name appears in all titles.

checks.name: Meta descriptions for all indexable pages
checks.description: Every indexable page must have a unique meta description of 120–160 characters that includes a value proposition and soft CTA.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites leave meta descriptions blank or auto-generate them from the first paragraph.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All indexable pages have unique meta descriptions 120–160 chars. No description is a truncated copy of the page H1.

checks.name: Open Graph and Twitter card tags complete
checks.description: All social share metadata must be present: og:title, og:description, og:image (1200×630px), og:url, twitter:card.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently have incomplete OG tags, causing broken or unbranded social previews.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Social share preview verified in Twitter Card Validator and LinkedIn Post Inspector. Custom og:image present on all key pages.

checks.name: Structured data for organisation
checks.description: JSON-LD Organisation schema must be present with name, url, logo, sameAs (social profiles), and contactPoint.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian-style template sites have no structured data markup of any kind.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Google Rich Results Test returns valid Organisation schema with zero errors.

checks.name: Canonical tags on all pages
checks.description: Every page must include a self-referencing canonical tag to prevent duplicate content indexing.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently serve the same content on multiple URLs without canonicalisation.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All pages have <link rel='canonical'> pointing to the preferred URL. Verified via Screaming Frog crawl.

checks.name: XML sitemap submitted and valid
checks.description: A machine-readable XML sitemap must be present at /sitemap.xml and submitted to Google Search Console.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites often generate malformed sitemaps or omit them entirely.
checks.acceptance_criteria: sitemap.xml validates against the sitemap protocol. All canonical URLs present. Submitted to and confirmed by Google Search Console.

checks.name: Named customer logos with permission
checks.description: Social proof logos must be real, named companies with documented permission to use the logo — not placeholder greyscale shapes.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian displays no named customer proof. Generic AI landing pages use placeholder logo rows with no verifiable customers.
checks.acceptance_criteria: ≥4 named customer logos present. Written permission documented for each. Logos link to a case study or testimonial.

checks.name: Testimonials are attributed and specific
checks.description: Every testimonial must include a real full name, job title, company, and a specific outcome or use case — not a vague compliment.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites use anonymous or first-name-only testimonials with generic praise that could apply to any product.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All testimonials have full name, verifiable job title, and company. ≥1 sentence per testimonial references a specific metric or outcome.

checks.name: Team page with real photos and bios
checks.description: Key team members must have professional photos (not AI-generated), real LinkedIn profiles, and bios that establish domain credibility.
checks.benchmark_gap: Many AI startup sites have no team page or use stock/AI-generated headshots that erode trust.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Team page lists ≥5 employees with real photos, linked LinkedIn profiles, and bios ≥40 words establishing relevant experience.

checks.name: Privacy policy is current and complete
checks.description: Privacy policy must cover GDPR/CCPA requirements, be dated within the last 12 months, and be written in plain language.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites frequently ship with boilerplate privacy policies that don't reflect their actual data practices.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Privacy policy covers: data collected, purpose, retention, third-party sharing, user rights, and contact. Reviewed by legal within 12 months.

checks.name: Security posture is visible
checks.description: For a B2B AI company, visible security signals (SOC 2, encryption standards, responsible disclosure) are table stakes.
checks.benchmark_gap: Venturian makes no mention of security, compliance, or data handling — a critical gap for enterprise buyers.
checks.acceptance_criteria: Security page or section present. ≥1 compliance certification mentioned with verification link. Responsible disclosure policy accessible from footer.

checks.name: Press and media coverage linked
checks.description: Credible third-party coverage (TechCrunch, The Information, Bloomberg, etc.) must be linked to, not just logged.
checks.benchmark_gap: Template sites list publication logos in a press bar without linking to actual articles.
checks.acceptance_criteria: All press logos link to the specific article. Links open in new tab. Articles are ≤18 months old.
